mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal a strengthening labor market, which drives higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC and post-COVID recoveries saw strong equity performance alongside payrolls growth.
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative median 20-day S&P 500 returns over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate indicates a sharp economic contraction is underway, triggering recession fears and causing investors to de-risk portfolios, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month low.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and decelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signal a stagflationary environment. This compresses corporate profit margins and valuation multiples, driving negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY % change > 3% and INDPRO YoY % change < 1%
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflationary period was characterized by poor equity performance.
failure_condition: The interaction signal's walk-forward Rank IC remains statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate is above the neutral rate, any further increase signals restrictive policy that slows economic activity and increases the discount rate for future earnings, causing negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The 2018 equity market correction was partially attributed to Fed tightening past the perceived neutral rate.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has a positive hit rate (>55%) for 20-day forward returns when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which is the primary driver of the US economy. This leads to upward revisions in corporate revenue forecasts and positive short-term equity performance.
failure_condition: The signal based on the 3-month change in real retail sales (RSAFS deflated by CPIAUCSL) fails to achieve a positive Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A large positive surprise in monthly housing starts (HOUST) signals strong household formation and confidence in the economic outlook, boosting cyclical sectors and leading to positive equity returns over a very short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a key leading indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: The median 5-day forward S&P 500 return is negative in the week following the release of a qualifying HOUST figure.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a very low unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an overheating economy where the central bank is actively trying to curb growth, marking the end of the economic cycle and leading to negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Late-cycle periods like 1999-2000 and 2006-2007 exhibited these characteristics before major downturns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy manufacturing and supply side of the economy, translating into higher corporate profits and supporting higher equity valuations.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in INDPRO has a negative correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.
